questions.prompt: What is the main advantage of DTL over pure diode logic?
options: Signal restoration — the BJT provides proper HIGH and LOW outputs
questions.correctAnswer: Signal restoration — the BJT provides proper HIGH and LOW outputs
questions.explanation: Pure diode logic degrades signal levels with each stage (≈0.8V drop). DTL adds a BJT inverter that restores output levels to near Vcc (HIGH) or near 0.2V (LOW), regardless of input degradation. This is called signal restoration.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of the two series diodes D1 and D2 in the DTL gate?
options: To create a voltage offset that prevents false turn-ON of the transistor
To protect the transistor from overvoltage
questions.correctAnswer: To create a voltage offset that prevents false turn-ON of the transistor
questions.explanation: When an input is LOW, the common node voltage is only ≈0.9V. Without D1 and D2, this might be enough to turn ON the BJT. The two diodes add 2 × 0.7V = 1.4V offset, requiring at least 2.0V at the node to turn ON the transistor. This ensures clean switching.

questions.prompt: What is the collector current IC when all inputs are HIGH? (Vcc=5V, RC=2.2kΩ, VCE,sat=0.2V)
questions.explanation: IC = (Vcc - VCE,sat) / RC = (5 - 0.2) / 2200 = 4.8 / 2200 = 2.182 mA. The collector current is determined by the supply voltage, collector resistance, and saturation voltage.

questions.prompt: What is the base current IB when all inputs are HIGH?
questions.explanation: IB = I1 - I2 = 0.56 mA - 0.16 mA = 0.4 mA. The current I1 enters from the 5kΩ resistor, and I2 = VBE,sat/RB = 0.8/5k = 0.16 mA exits through the base resistor. The remainder goes into the base.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum hFE required for the transistor to remain in saturation?
questions.explanation: At the saturation threshold: IB = IC/hFE,min. So hFE,min = IC/IB = 2.182/0.4 = 5.46 ≈ 5. Any transistor with hFE ≥ 5 will reliably saturate. Most practical BJTs have hFE > 50, so this condition is easily met.

questions.prompt: When one input is LOW (0V), what is the voltage at the common anode node?
questions.explanation: The LOW input is at ≈ 0.2V (representing binary 0). The conducting diode adds VD,ON ≈ 0.7V: Vi = 0.2V + 0.7V = 0.9V at the common node. This is the voltage available to drive D1, D2, and the base.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum voltage needed at the common node to turn ON the transistor?
questions.explanation: To turn ON the transistor, the voltage must overcome: 2 × Vγ (for D1 and D2) + VBE,ON = 2 × 0.6V + 0.8V = 2.0V. Since the node is only at 0.9V when an input is LOW, the transistor stays firmly OFF.

questions.prompt: What is VCE,sat for a BJT in deep saturation?
questions.explanation: When a BJT is driven into saturation (IB well above IC/hFE), the collector-emitter voltage drops to approximately 0.2V. This is the saturation voltage VCE,sat — the minimum voltage the transistor can achieve at its collector.

questions.prompt: What is the node voltage VP when all inputs are HIGH? (VD,ON=0.7V, VBE,sat=0.8V)
questions.explanation: VP must supply the voltage drops across D1, D2, and VBE: VP = 2 × VD,ON + VBE,sat = 2(0.7) + 0.8 = 1.4 + 0.8 = 2.2V. This determines I1 = (Vcc - VP)/R = (5 - 2.2)/5k = 0.56 mA.

questions.prompt: What logic function does the DTL gate implement?
questions.explanation: The diode section implements AND (output HIGH only when all inputs HIGH), and the BJT inverts the result. AND + inversion = NAND. The output is LOW only when ALL inputs are HIGH. If any input is LOW, the output is HIGH.

questions.prompt: Why is this called Diode-Transistor Logic (DTL)?
options: It combines diode logic (for the AND function) with a transistor (for inversion and amplification)
It uses diodes instead of transistors
questions.correctAnswer: It combines diode logic (for the AND function) with a transistor (for inversion and amplification)
questions.explanation: DTL literally combines two technologies: DIODE logic handles the multi-input AND function, and a TRANSISTOR provides inversion plus signal restoration. The transistor is the key improvement over pure diode logic, giving clean output levels.
